faqs.question: Why are flies so common in Pelham Bay properties?
faqs.answer: Pelham Bay's location near water bodies like Eastchester Bay, combined with the expansive Pelham Bay Park and organic waste from residential and commercial areas, creates perfect breeding conditions for flies. The neighborhood's mix of restaurants, food markets, and proximity to wetlands contributes to higher fly populations year-round.
